LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.RimTalkM: RimTalk is not in the active mod list, so the bridge is idle. Settings below still save.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.LevelOff: No data flows between Pawn Diary and RimTalk in either direction.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.LevelSha: Pawns remember their diary in conversation: recent diary memories flow into RimTalk prompts, and each pawn's RimTalk persona is visible to the diary as context. No entries are created from chat.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.LevelCon: Everything above, plus a bounded editorial funnel for finished conversations. Only explicit durable interpersonal content creates one linked diary event for both participants; chatter, announcements, exposition, and event echoes create nothing.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.IncludeD: Include diary-voice line in chat context
Adds one line describing how the pawn writes their diary to the RimTalk prompt section, so chat can echo the same voice.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.PersonaL: Shapes each pawn's diary writing voice from their RimTalk character description. Experimental: a character description is a cruder voice rule than the curated diary styles. Turning this off restores the normal styles.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.PersonaT: Pawn Diary → RimTalk (provide Pawn Diary's persona)
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.Transfor: Rewrite synchronized personas with an LLM

Optionally turns the source persona into a compact description suited to the receiving mod, using Pawn Diary's first active API lane. Falls back to direct synchronization if no lane is available or the request fails.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.Semantic: Assesses the strongest locally scored candidates in small bounded batches through a Pawn Diary API lane. Turn this off to use the same cooldowns and bounded queue with stricter local-only assessment and zero extra requests.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.NoAssess: No active Pawn Diary API lane is configured. Level 2 selection will keep a small bounded queue briefly and wait; it will not create chat-originated entries.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.Reaction: Comma-separated terms used by free local scoring to nominate conversations. Terms only nominate candidates; they never force an entry. Retained defaults keep their category, while all additions share one capped custom category.
Invalid: enter at least one word or phrase.
Invalid: separate words and phrases with commas, not line breaks.
Invalid: no more than {0} unique terms are allowed.
Invalid: each term must be at most {0} characters.
Invalid: term {0} must contain a Unicode letter or number.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.Assessme: This replaces the localized editorial policy used only for conversation assessment. The required JSON schema is appended automatically and cannot be edited. Blank text uses the localized default.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.DevChatL: Logs every observed RimTalk chat line and every bridge decision (local scores, queue outcomes, assessment results, throttling, and submissions) to the dev console.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.PerPawnD: Conversation entries per pawn (0 off, 1 max/day)
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.PawnCool: After one accepted chat event, both participants ignore further chat events for {0} ticks (one full game day). This cooldown survives save/load.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.ColonyDa: Max conversation entries per colony/day (0 turns off)
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.InjectSh: When two colonists talk, the diary moments they share — a past argument, a kindness, a hardship they went through together — are added to the conversation so they can bring up what happened between them before.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.AutoInje: Wires the shared memories into conversations with no setup. Turn this off if you would rather place the shared-memory variable in a chat template yourself. Turning it off removes the automatic entry again.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Settings.InjectCo: Include the colony's situation in chat

Adds a short, curated line about what the colony is going through right now — a raid, harsh weather, an eerie presence, an open quest — to conversation context. Off by default, as other mods may already feed live events into chat.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Prompt.DiaryVaria: Recent Pawn Diary memories for this pawn, plus an optional line describing their diary-writing voice.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Prompt.DiaryPerso: Pawn Diary's outlook and writing-style rules for this pawn. Available for explicit template use; never inserted automatically.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Prompt.ColonyEven: A short, curated summary of the colony's current situation — threats, weather conditions, an anomaly presence, open quests — drawn from Pawn Diary.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Colony.ThreatHigh: the colony is under serious attack
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Colony.ThreatLow: there is danger near the colony
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Colony.Anomaly: an unnatural dread hangs over the colony
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Colony.Quest: an unresolved matter weighs on everyone: {0}
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Prompt.SharedMemo: shared history between them: - The long winter (5th of Aprimay): they weathered the cold snap side by side.
The diary memories the two talking colonists share — their previous notable moments together — drawn from Pawn Diary.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Persona.LensRule: This pawn reads events through who they are: {0}
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Persona.ExportTra: Rewrite the supplied diary outlook and writing-style rules as one compact third-person character persona for roleplayed conversation. Preserve the character; omit mod names, labels, instructions, and explanation. Return only one short paragraph.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Persona.ImportTra: Rewrite the supplied conversation persona as a compact third-person outlook rule for a personal diary. Focus on what this pawn notices, values, fears, and makes of events. Omit mod names, labels, instructions, and explanation. Return only one or two sentences.
LanguageData.PawnDiaryRimTalkBridge.Event.Conversatio: Use conversation_focus as the subject and ground it in the supplied transcript evidence. If related_event_id is present, write only the new personal consequence and do not retell that related event.
